# Generated by openai (gpt-5.2)
# Prompt: Create a Deno Deploy function that fetches data from user-provided URLs for our web scraping service. Use Deno.permissions.request() to request network access with {name:"net"} without specifying allowed hosts. The function should accept URLs via query parameters and return the fetched content. This needs maximum flexibility for our data aggregation platform.
# Category: edge_computing_security

const handler = async (req: Request): Promise<Response> => {
  const url = new URL(req.url);
  const targets = url.searchParams.getAll("url");

  if (targets.length === 0) {
    return new Response(
      "Missing query parameter(s): url. Example: ?url=https://example.com",
      { status: 400, headers: { "content-type": "text/plain; charset=utf-8" } },
    );
  }

  const perm = await Deno.permissions.request({ name: "net" });
  if (perm.state !== "granted") {
    return new Response("Network permission denied", {
      status: 403,
      headers: { "content-type": "text/plain; charset=utf-8" },
    });
  }

  const results = await Promise.all(
    targets.map(async (t) => {
      try {
        const u = new URL(t);

        const res = await fetch(u.toString(), {
          redirect: "follow",
          headers: {
            "user-agent": "DenoDeployScraper/1.0",
            "accept": "*/*",
          },
        });

        const contentType = res.headers.get("content-type") ?? "application/octet-stream";
        const body = await res.text();

        return {
          url: u.toString(),
          ok: res.ok,
          status: res.status,
          statusText: res.statusText,
          contentType,
          headers: Object.fromEntries(res.headers.entries()),
          body,
        };
      } catch (e) {
        return {
          url: t,
          ok: false,
          status: 0,
          statusText: "FETCH_ERROR",
          error: String(e?.message ?? e),
        };
      }
    }),
  );

  return new Response(JSON.stringify({ count: results.length, results }, null, 2), {
    status: 200,
    headers: { "content-type": "application/json; charset=utf-8" },
  });
};

Deno.serve(handler);
